description: Letter from Shelomo b. Ḥayyim b. Shelomo to Avraham ha-Sar b. Natan ‘the Seventh’. Dating: Ca. 1100 CE. Information from CUDL. There is a rhymed Hebrew introduction (~10 lines) and a Judaeo-Arabic body (~6 lines plus margins). The bulk of the letter is taken up with good wishes for the addressee's recovery from an illness. It briefly touches on financial dealings, e.g., the writer has obtained nearly 4 dinars of the money owed to the addressee by Ibn Shuwayʿ. The writer twice sends regards to the Nagid Mevorakh, urging Avraham to tell him of Shelomo's "iftiqād" (preoccupation/solicitude, especially during illness) for him. ASE.
tags: Illness, illness: iftiqad, illness letter 969-1517, illness: public figure
description_he: מכתב משלמה ב. חיים ב. שלמה לאברהם השר ב. נתן השביעי. תאריך: כ-1100 לספירה. הקדמה מחורזת בעברית וגוף ביהודית-ערבית. רובו ברכות להחלמה מהמחלה ועניינים כספיים קצרים
